summary: Phenylalanine is directly converted to tyrosine by phenylalanine hydroxylase; this conversion makes phenylalanine the only amino acid that readily forms tyrosine among those considered clinically significant. The pathway underscores a critical link between these two amino acids in protein metabolism.
